rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC SAND & GRAVEL, INC. VS. MITCHELL ENGINEERING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Strike The First Amended Complaint In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, Arpil 8, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING/OBAYASHI CORPORATION, LUMBERMENS MUTUAL CASUALTY COMPANY, NATIONAL FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF HARTFORD, UNITED STATES FIDELITY AND GUARANTY COMPANY, ST.PAUL FIRE AND MARINE INSURANCE COMPANY, and, NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE OF PITTSBURG'S, Motion To Strike The First Amended Complaint In Intervention. Motion to strike is denied. No showing that the first amended complaint does not comply with CCP section 387. Court order of April 28, 2008 makes no substantive rulings or constructions on first amended complaint, but merely grants leave to intervene. Sufficient facts pled to support a controversy between the rights of Pacific Sand & Gravel and of Bay Business Credit. =(302CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ELENA WILLIAMS et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, Arpil 8, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT O G SUPPLY, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Pursuant Code Of Civil Procedure Section 583.410. Off Calendar, per moving party. =(302CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY J JACQUES VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DBA SAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement Of Deft Ccsf;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, Arpil 8, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT LUCIA CHEN-LUFTIG, ROCHELLE REYES, and, CALIFORNIA EMERGENCY PHYSICIANS MEDICAL GROUP'S Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement Of Defendant CCSF. Motion to contest the Good Faith Settlement of defendant City and County of San Francisco is granted. City and County of San Francisco does not present sufficient evidence to approximate liability. See MattcoForge vs. Arthur Young, 38, Cal.App. 4th 1337, 1350, n.6. =(302CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ERIC KASTELL et al VS. SALVADOR CHAVEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, Arpil 8, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT SALVADOR CHAVEZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff to plead facts that show due diligence documents were false, why defendant would have a duty to disclose a $1,000,000 tax liability, or whether this liability would make defendant's company unhealthy financially. =(302CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ENGELBERG et al VS. ELLEN TSANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, Arpil 8, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT ELLEN TSANG, TRUSTEE OF THE ARZA TRUST'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court treats this as a motion for reconsideration of the earlier demurrer which was overruled on March 5, 2009. (CCP section 1008). Motion for reconsideration is denied. Defendant fails to present any new law, facts, or circumstances. =(302CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER LIM-HU ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED VS CARTER'S RETAIL INC ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, Arpil 8, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT CARTER RETAIL INC.'S Motion for Change of Venue. Motion is denied. When the action was commenced, defendant was a foreign corporation that had not designated a principal place of business anywhere in California. =(302CWW/VC)
